name: Apache Kafka
what_it_does: Distributed event streaming platform for high-throughput, fault-tolerant publish-subscribe messaging and real-time data pipelines.
use_cases: Stream 1M+ events/second from IoT devices to multiple downstream consumers with guaranteed ordering
Build event sourcing architecture where all state changes are captured as Kafka events
Implement CDC (Change Data Capture) pipeline from PostgreSQL to Kafka using Debezium connector
Fan-out user activity events to analytics, recommendations, and fraud detection services simultaneously
Buffer spiky write traffic before database ingestion using Kafka as a durable write-ahead log
not_for: Simple task queues with work distribution — use RabbitMQ or Redis queues
Request-reply RPC patterns — Kafka is not designed for synchronous communication
Small-scale messaging under 10K messages/day where operational complexity exceeds value
best_when: Processing millions of events per second with durable, replayable event log across multiple consumer groups.
avoid_when: Operational team lacks Kafka expertise — it requires significant tuning and monitoring investment.

auth.notes: SASL/PLAIN, SASL/SCRAM-SHA-256/512, or mTLS for broker auth; ACLs for topic-level authorization

pricing.notes: OSS free; managed options via Confluent, MSK, Aiven, Upstash

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted; throughput scales with partition count and hardware

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Client library errors are descriptive; broker errors returned as error codes requiring lookup
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotent producer (enable.idempotence=true) + transactional API for exactly-once semantics
known_agent_gotchas: Partition count is immutable after topic creation — under-partitioning is a permanent bottleneck
Consumer group rebalancing (partition reassignment) pauses all consumers — minimize rebalance frequency
KRaft mode (ZooKeeper-free) is default in 3.x but migration from ZK clusters requires specific procedure
Producer acks=all with min.insync.replicas=2 required for durability — acks=1 default can lose data on broker failure
Consumer lag monitoring requires external tooling (Burrow, Kafka UI) — no built-in lag alerting

security_score_components.security_notes: mTLS + ACLs provide strong security; default configs are insecure — harden before production
